NKJV.1: Paul, a bondservant of Jesus Christ, called to be an apostle, separated to the gospel of God
NKJV.2: which He promised before through His prophets in the Holy Scriptures,
NKJV.3: concerning His Son Jesus Christ our Lord, who was born of the seed of David according to the flesh,
NKJV.4: and declared to be the Son of God with power according to the Spirit of holiness, by the resurrection from the dead.
NKJV.5: Through Him we have received grace and apostleship for obedience to the faith among all nations for His name,
NKJV.6: among whom you also are the called of Jesus Christ;
NKJV.7: To all who are in Rome, beloved of God, called to be saints: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
NKJV.8: First, I thank my God through Jesus Christ for you all, that your faith is spoken of throughout the whole world.
NKJV.9: For God is my witness, whom I serve with my spirit in the gospel of His Son, that without ceasing I make mention of you always in my prayers,
NKJV.10: making request if, by some means, now at last I may find a way in the will of God to come to you.
NKJV.11: For I long to see you, that I may impart to you some spiritual gift, so that you may be established—
NKJV.12: that is, that I may be encouraged together with you by the mutual faith both of you and me.
NKJV.13: Now I do not want you to be unaware, brethren, that I often planned to come to you (but was hindered until now), that I might have some fruit among you also, just as among the other Gentiles.
NKJV.14: I am a debtor both to Greeks and to barbarians, both to wise and to unwise.
NKJV.15: So, as much as is in me, I am ready to preach the gospel to you who are in Rome also.
NKJV.16: For I am not ashamed of the gospel of Christ, for it is the power of God to salvation for everyone who believes, for the Jew first and also for the Greek.
NKJV.17: For in it the righteousness of God is revealed from faith to faith; as it is written, “The just shall live by faith.”
NKJV.18: For the wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness and unrighteousness of men, who suppress the truth in unrighteousness,
NKJV.19: because what may be known of God is manifest in them, for God has shown it to them.
NKJV.20: For since the creation of the world His invisible attributes are clearly seen, being understood by the things that are made, even His eternal power and Godhead, so that they are without excuse,
NKJV.21: because, although they knew God, they did not glorify Him as God, nor were thankful, but became futile in their thoughts, and their foolish hearts were darkened.
NKJV.22: Professing to be wise, they became fools,
NKJV.23: and changed the glory of the incorruptible God into an image made like corruptible man—and birds and four-footed animals and creeping things.
NKJV.24: Therefore God also gave them up to uncleanness, in the lusts of their hearts, to dishonor their bodies among themselves,
NKJV.25: who exchanged the truth of God for the lie, and worshiped and served the creature rather than the Creator, who is blessed forever. Amen.
NKJV.26: For this reason God gave them up to vile passions. For even their women exchanged the natural use for what is against nature.
NKJV.27: Likewise also the men, leaving the natural use of the woman, burned in their lust for one another, men with men committing what is shameful, and receiving in themselves the penalty of their error which was due.
NKJV.28: And even as they did not like to retain God in their knowledge, God gave them over to a debased mind, to do those things which are not fitting;
NKJV.29: being filled with all unrighteousness, sexual immorality, wickedness, covetousness, maliciousness; full of envy, murder, strife, deceit, evil-mindedness; they are whisperers,
NKJV.30: backbiters, haters of God, violent, proud, boasters, inventors of evil things, disobedient to parents,
NKJV.32: who, knowing the righteous judgment of God, that those who practice such things are deserving of death, not only do the same but also approve of those who practice them.

ESV.1: Paul, a servant  of Christ Jesus, called to be an apostle, set apart for the gospel of God,
ESV.2: which he promised beforehand through his prophets in the holy Scriptures,
ESV.3: concerning his Son, who was descended from David  according to the flesh
ESV.4: and was declared to be the Son of God in power according to the Spirit of holiness by his resurrection from the dead, Jesus Christ our Lord,
ESV.5: through whom we have received grace and apostleship to bring about the obedience of faith for the sake of his name among all the nations,
ESV.6: including you who are called to belong to Jesus Christ,
ESV.7: To all those in Rome who are loved by God and called to be saints: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
ESV.8: First, I thank my God through Jesus Christ for all of you, because your faith is proclaimed in all the world.
ESV.9: For God is my witness, whom I serve with my spirit in the gospel of his Son, that without ceasing I mention you
ESV.10: always in my prayers, asking that somehow by God's will I may now at last succeed in coming to you.
ESV.11: For I long to see you, that I may impart to you some spiritual gift to strengthen you —
ESV.12: that is, that we may be mutually encouraged by each other's faith, both yours and mine.
ESV.13: I do not want you to be unaware, brothers,  that I have often intended to come to you (but thus far have been prevented), in order that I may reap some harvest among you as well as among the rest of the Gentiles.
ESV.14: I am under obligation both to Greeks and to barbarians,  both to the wise and to the foolish.
ESV.15: So I am eager to preach the gospel to you also who are in Rome.
ESV.16: For I am not ashamed of the gospel, for it is the power of God for salvation to everyone who believes, to the Jew first and also to the Greek.
ESV.17: For in it the righteousness of God is revealed from faith for faith,  as it is written, “The righteous shall live by faith.”
ESV.18: For the wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness and unrighteousness of men, who by their unrighteousness suppress the truth.
ESV.19: For what can be known about God is plain to them, because God has shown it to them.
ESV.20: For his invisible attributes, namely, his eternal power and divine nature, have been clearly perceived, ever since the creation of the world,  in the things that have been made. So they are without excuse.
ESV.21: For although they knew God, they did not honor him as God or give thanks to him, but they became futile in their thinking, and their foolish hearts were darkened.
ESV.22: Claiming to be wise, they became fools,
ESV.23: and exchanged the glory of the immortal God for images resembling mortal man and birds and animals and creeping things.
ESV.24: Therefore God gave them up in the lusts of their hearts to impurity, to the dishonoring of their bodies among themselves,
ESV.25: because they exchanged the truth about God for a lie and worshiped and served the creature rather than the Creator, who is blessed forever! Amen.
ESV.26: For this reason God gave them up to dishonorable passions. For their women exchanged natural relations for those that are contrary to nature;
ESV.27: and the men likewise gave up natural relations with women and were consumed with passion for one another, men committing shameless acts with men and receiving in themselves the due penalty for their error.
ESV.28: And since they did not see fit to acknowledge God, God gave them up to a debased mind to do what ought not to be done.
ESV.29: They were filled with all manner of unrighteousness, evil, covetousness, malice. They are full of envy, murder, strife, deceit, maliciousness. They are gossips,
ESV.30: slanderers, haters of God, insolent, haughty, boastful, inventors of evil, disobedient to parents,
ESV.32: Though they know God's righteous decree that those who practice such things deserve to die, they not only do them but give approval to those who practice them.

NASB.1: Paul, a bond-servant of Christ Jesus, called [as] an apostle, set apart for the gospel of God,
NASB.2: which He promised beforehand through His prophets in the holy Scriptures,
NASB.3: concerning His Son, who was born of a descendant of David according to the flesh,
NASB.4: who was declared the Son of God with power by the resurrection from the dead, according to the Spirit of holiness, Jesus Christ our Lord,
NASB.5: through whom we have received grace and apostleship to bring about [the] obedience of faith among all the Gentiles for His name's sake,
NASB.6: among whom you also are the called of Jesus Christ;
NASB.7: to all who are beloved of God in Rome, called [as] saints: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
NASB.8: First, I thank my God through Jesus Christ for you all, because your faith is being proclaimed throughout the whole world.
NASB.9: For God, whom I serve in my spirit in the [preaching of the] gospel of His Son, is my witness [as to] how unceasingly I make mention of you,
NASB.10: always in my prayers making request, if perhaps now at last by the will of God I may succeed in coming to you.
NASB.11: For I long to see you so that I may impart some spiritual gift to you, that you may be established;
NASB.12: that is, that I may be encouraged together with you [while] among you, each of us by the other's faith, both yours and mine.
NASB.13: I do not want you to be unaware, brethren, that often I have planned to come to you (and have been prevented so far) so that I may obtain some fruit among you also, even as among the rest of the Gentiles.
NASB.14: I am under obligation both to Greeks and to barbarians, both to the wise and to the foolish.
NASB.15: So, for my part, I am eager to preach the gospel to you also who are in Rome.
NASB.16: For I am not ashamed of the gospel, for it is the power of God for salvation to everyone who believes, to the Jew first and also to the Greek.
NASB.17: For in it [the] righteousness of God is revealed from faith to faith; as it is written, 'BUT THE RIGHTEOUS [man] SHALL LIVE BY FAITH.'
NASB.18: For the wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness and unrighteousness of men who suppress the truth in unrighteousness,
NASB.19: because that which is known about God is evident within them; for God made it evident to them.
NASB.20: For since the creation of the world His invisible attributes, His eternal power and divine nature, have been clearly seen, being understood through what has been made, so that they are without excuse.
NASB.21: For even though they knew God, they did not honor Him as God or give thanks, but they became futile in their speculations, and their foolish heart was darkened.
NASB.22: Professing to be wise, they became fools,
NASB.23: and exchanged the glory of the incorruptible God for an image in the form of corruptible man and of birds and four-footed animals and crawling creatures.
NASB.24: Therefore God gave them over in the lusts of their hearts to impurity, so that their bodies would be dishonored among them.
NASB.25: For they exchanged the truth of God for a lie, and worshiped and served the creature rather than the Creator, who is blessed forever. Amen.
NASB.26: For this reason God gave them over to degrading passions; for their women exchanged the natural function for that which is unnatural,
NASB.27: and in the same way also the men abandoned the natural function of the woman and burned in their desire toward one another, men with men committing indecent acts and receiving in their own persons the due penalty of their error.
NASB.28: And just as they did not see fit to acknowledge God any longer, God gave them over to a depraved mind, to do those things which are not proper,
NASB.29: being filled with all unrighteousness, wickedness, greed, evil; full of envy, murder, strife, deceit, malice; [they are] gossips,
NASB.30: slanderers, haters of God, insolent, arrogant, boastful, inventors of evil, disobedient to parents,
NASB.32: and although they know the ordinance of God, that those who practice such things are worthy of death, they not only do the same, but also give hearty approval to those who practice them.

NLT.1: This letter is from Paul, a slave of Christ Jesus, chosen by God to be an apostle and sent out to preach his Good News.
NLT.2: God promised this Good News long ago through his prophets in the holy Scriptures.
NLT.3: The Good News is about his Son. In his earthly life he was born into King David’s family line,
NLT.4: and he was shown to be the Son of God when he was raised from the dead by the power of the Holy Spirit. He is Jesus Christ our Lord.
NLT.5: Through Christ, God has given us the privilege and authority as apostles to tell Gentiles everywhere what God has done for them, so that they will believe and obey him, bringing glory to his name.
NLT.6: And you are included among those Gentiles who have been called to belong to Jesus Christ.
NLT.7: I am writing to all of you in Rome who are loved by God and are called to be his own holy people. May God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ give you grace and peace.
NLT.8: Let me say first that I thank my God through Jesus Christ for all of you, because your faith in him is being talked about all over the world.
NLT.9: God knows how often I pray for you. Day and night I bring you and your needs in prayer to God, whom I serve with all my heart by spreading the Good News about his Son.
NLT.10: One of the things I always pray for is the opportunity, God willing, to come at last to see you.
NLT.11: For I long to visit you so I can bring you some spiritual gift that will help you grow strong in the Lord.
NLT.12: When we get together, I want to encourage you in your faith, but I also want to be encouraged by yours.
NLT.13: I want you to know, dear brothers and sisters, that I planned many times to visit you, but I was prevented until now. I want to work among you and see spiritual fruit, just as I have seen among other Gentiles.
NLT.14: For I have a great sense of obligation to people in both the civilized world and the rest of the world, to the educated and uneducated alike.
NLT.15: So I am eager to come to you in Rome, too, to preach the Good News.
NLT.16: For I am not ashamed of this Good News about Christ. It is the power of God at work, saving everyone who believes — the Jew first and also the Gentile.
NLT.17: This Good News tells us how God makes us right in his sight. This is accomplished from start to finish by faith. As the Scriptures say, “It is through faith that a righteous person has life.”
NLT.18: But God shows his anger from heaven against all sinful, wicked people who suppress the truth by their wickedness.
NLT.19: They know the truth about God because he has made it obvious to them.
NLT.20: For ever since the world was created, people have seen the earth and sky. Through everything God made, they can clearly see his invisible qualities — his eternal power and divine nature. So they have no excuse for not knowing God.
NLT.21: Yes, they knew God, but they wouldn’t worship him as God or even give him thanks. And they began to think up foolish ideas of what God was like. As a result, their minds became dark and confused.
NLT.22: Claiming to be wise, they instead became utter fools.
NLT.23: And instead of worshiping the glorious, ever-living God, they worshiped idols made to look like mere people and birds and animals and reptiles.
NLT.24: So God abandoned them to do whatever shameful things their hearts desired. As a result, they did vile and degrading things with each other’s bodies.
NLT.25: They traded the truth about God for a lie. So they worshiped and served the things God created instead of the Creator himself, who is worthy of eternal praise! Amen.
NLT.26: That is why God abandoned them to their shameful desires. Even the women turned against the natural way to have sex and instead indulged in sex with each other.
NLT.27: And the men, instead of having normal sexual relations with women, burned with lust for each other. Men did shameful things with other men, and as a result of this sin, they suffered within themselves the penalty they deserved.
NLT.28: Since they thought it foolish to acknowledge God, he abandoned them to their foolish thinking and let them do things that should never be done.
NLT.29: Their lives became full of every kind of wickedness, sin, greed, hate, envy, murder, quarreling, deception, malicious behavior, and gossip.
NLT.30: They are backstabbers, haters of God, insolent, proud, and boastful. They invent new ways of sinning, and they disobey their parents.
NLT.31: They refuse to understand, break their promises, are heartless, and have no mercy.
NLT.32: They know God’s justice requires that those who do these things deserve to die, yet they do them anyway. Worse yet, they encourage others to do them, too.

WEB.1: Paul, a servant of Jesus Christ, called to be an apostle, set apart for the Good News of God,
WEB.2: which he promised before through his prophets in the holy Scriptures,
WEB.3: concerning his Son, who was born of the offspring  of David according to the flesh,
WEB.4: who was declared to be the Son of God with power, according to the Spirit of holiness, by the resurrection from the dead, Jesus Christ our Lord,
WEB.5: through whom we received grace and apostleship, for obedience of faith among all the nations, for his name’s sake;
WEB.6: among whom you are also called to belong to Jesus Christ;
WEB.7: to all who are in Rome, beloved of God, called to be saints: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
WEB.8: First, I thank my God through Jesus Christ for all of you, that your faith is proclaimed throughout the whole world.
WEB.9: For God is my witness, whom I serve in my spirit in the Good News of his Son, how unceasingly I make mention of you always in my prayers,
WEB.10: requesting, if by any means now at last I may be prospered by the will of God to come to you.
WEB.11: For I long to see you, that I may impart to you some spiritual gift, to the end that you may be established;
WEB.12: that is, that I with you may be encouraged in you, each of us by the other’s faith, both yours and mine.
WEB.13: Now I don’t desire to have you unaware, brothers, that I often planned to come to you, and was hindered so far, that I might have some fruit among you also, even as among the rest of the Gentiles.
WEB.14: I am debtor both to Greeks and to foreigners, both to the wise and to the foolish.
WEB.15: So, as much as is in me, I am eager to preach the Good News to you also who are in Rome.
WEB.16: For I am not ashamed of the Good News of Christ, for it is the power of God for salvation for everyone who believes; for the Jew first, and also for the Greek.
WEB.17: For in it is revealed God’s righteousness from faith to faith. As it is written, “But the righteous shall live by faith.”
WEB.18: For the wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness and unrighteousness of men, who suppress the truth in unrighteousness,
WEB.19: because that which is known of God is revealed in them, for God revealed it to them.
WEB.20: For the invisible things of him since the creation of the world are clearly seen, being perceived through the things that are made, even his everlasting power and divinity; that they may be without excuse.
WEB.21: Because, knowing God, they didn’t glorify him as God, neither gave thanks, but became vain in their reasoning, and their senseless heart was darkened.
WEB.22: Professing themselves to be wise, they became fools,
WEB.23: and traded the glory of the incorruptible God for the likeness of an image of corruptible man, and of birds, and four-footed animals, and creeping things.
WEB.24: Therefore God also gave them up in the lusts of their hearts to uncleanness, that their bodies should be dishonored among themselves,
WEB.25: who exchanged the truth of God for a lie, and worshiped and served the creature rather than the Creator, who is blessed forever. Amen.
WEB.26: For this reason, God gave them up to vile passions. For their women changed the natural function into that which is against nature.
WEB.27: Likewise also the men, leaving the natural function of the woman, burned in their lust toward one another, men doing what is inappropriate with men, and receiving in themselves the due penalty of their error.
WEB.28: Even as they refused to have God in their knowledge, God gave them up to a reprobate mind, to do those things which are not fitting;
WEB.29: being filled with all unrighteousness, sexual immorality, wickedness, covetousness, malice; full of envy, murder, strife, deceit, evil habits, secret slanderers,
WEB.30: backbiters, hateful to God, insolent, haughty, boastful, inventors of evil things, disobedient to parents,
WEB.31: without understanding, covenant breakers, without natural affection, unforgiving, unmerciful;
WEB.32: who, knowing the ordinance of God, that those who practice such things are worthy of death, not only do the same, but also approve of those who practice them.

CSB17.1: Paul, a servant of Christ Jesus, called as an apostle <sup>[1]</sup> <sup>ⓓ</sup> and set apart <sup>ⓔ</sup> for the gospel of God <sup>ⓕ</sup>  —
CSB17.2: which he promised beforehand <sup>ⓖ</sup> through his prophets <sup>ⓗ</sup> in the Holy Scriptures —
CSB17.3: concerning his Son, Jesus Christ our Lord, who was a descendant of David <sup>[2]</sup> <sup>ⓘ</sup> according to the flesh <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.4: and was appointed to be the powerful <sup>ⓚ</sup> Son of God <sup>ⓛ</sup> according to the Spirit of holiness <sup>[3]</sup> by the resurrection of the dead.
CSB17.5: Through him we have received grace and apostleship <sup>ⓜ</sup> to bring about <sup>[4]</sup> the obedience <sup>ⓝ</sup> of faith for the sake of his name among all the Gentiles, <sup>[5]</sup> <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.6: including you who are also called by Jesus Christ. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.7: To all who are in Rome, loved by God, <sup>ⓒ</sup> called <sup>ⓓ</sup> as saints. <sup>ⓔ</sup> Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
CSB17.8: First, I thank my God through Jesus Christ for all of you because the news of your faith <sup>[6]</sup> is being reported in all the world. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.9: God is my witness, <sup>ⓖ</sup> whom I serve with my spirit <sup>ⓗ</sup> in telling the good news about his Son — that I constantly mention you, <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.10: always asking in my prayers that if it is somehow in God’s will, I may now at last succeed in coming to you. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.11: For I want very much to see you, <sup>ⓚ</sup> so that I may impart to you some spiritual gift to strengthen you,
CSB17.12: that is, to be mutually encouraged by each other’s faith, both yours and mine.
CSB17.13: Now I don’t want you to be unaware, <sup>ⓛ</sup> brothers and sisters, that I often planned to come to you (but was prevented until now <sup>ⓜ</sup>) in order that I might have a fruitful ministry <sup>[7]</sup> among you, <sup>ⓝ</sup> just as I have had among the rest of the Gentiles.
CSB17.14: I am obligated both to Greeks and barbarians, <sup>[8]</sup> <sup>ⓐ</sup> both to the wise and the foolish.
CSB17.15: So I am eager to preach the gospel <sup>ⓑ</sup> to you also who are in Rome.
CSB17.16: For I am not ashamed of the gospel, <sup>[9]</sup> <sup>ⓒ</sup> because it is the power of God for salvation <sup>ⓓ</sup> to everyone who believes, first to the Jew, <sup>ⓔ</sup> and also to the Greek. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.17: For in it the righteousness of God is revealed from faith to faith, [10] ⓖ just as it is written: The righteous will live by faith. [11] [12] ⓗ
CSB17.18: For God’s wrath <sup>ⓘ</sup> is revealed from heaven against all godlessness and unrighteousness of people who by their unrighteousness suppress the truth, <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.19: since what can be known <sup>[13]</sup> about God is evident among them, <sup>ⓚ</sup> because God has shown it to them.
CSB17.20: For his invisible attributes, that is, his eternal power and divine nature, have been clearly seen since the creation of the world, <sup>ⓛ</sup> being understood through what he has made. <sup>ⓜ</sup> As a result, people are without excuse.
CSB17.21: For though they knew God, they did not glorify him as God or show gratitude. Instead, their thinking became worthless, and their senseless hearts were darkened. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.22: Claiming to be wise, they became fools <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.23: and exchanged the glory of the immortal God for images resembling mortal man, birds, four-footed animals, and reptiles. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.24: Therefore God delivered them over in the desires of their hearts <sup>ⓒ</sup> to sexual impurity, so that their bodies were degraded among themselves.
CSB17.25: They exchanged the truth of God for a lie, <sup>ⓓ</sup> and worshiped and served what has been created instead of the Creator, who is praised forever. <sup>ⓔ</sup> Amen.
CSB17.26: For this reason God delivered them over to disgraceful passions. <sup>ⓕ</sup> Their women <sup>[14]</sup> exchanged natural sexual relations <sup>[15]</sup> for unnatural ones.
CSB17.27: The men <sup>[16]</sup> in the same way also left natural relations with women and were inflamed in their lust for one another. Men committed shameless acts with men <sup>ⓖ</sup> and received in their own persons <sup>[17]</sup> the appropriate penalty of their error.
CSB17.28: And because they did not think it worthwhile to acknowledge God, God delivered them over to a corrupt mind so that they do what is not right.
CSB17.29: They are filled with all unrighteousness, <sup>[18]</sup> evil, greed, and wickedness. They are full of envy, murder, quarrels, deceit, and malice. They are gossips, <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.30: slanderers, God-haters, arrogant, proud, boastful, <sup>ⓘ</sup> inventors of evil, disobedient to parents, <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.32: Although they know God’s just sentence — that those who practice such things deserve to die <sup>[20]</sup> <sup>ⓛ</sup>  — they not only do them, but even applaud <sup>[21]</sup> <sup>ⓜ</sup> others who practice them.

AMP.1: Paul, a bond-servant of Christ Jesus, called as an apostle (special messenger, personally chosen representative), set apart for [preaching] the gospel of God [the good news of salvation],
AMP.2: which He promised beforehand through His prophets in the sacred Scriptures—
AMP.3: [the good news] regarding His Son, who, as to the flesh [His human nature], was born a descendant of David [to fulfill the covenant promises],
AMP.4: and [as to His divine nature] according to the Spirit of holiness was openly designated to be the Son of God with power [in a triumphant and miraculous way] by His resurrection from the dead: Jesus Christ our Lord.
AMP.5: It is through Him that we have received grace and [our] apostleship to promote obedience to the faith and make disciples for His name's sake among all the Gentiles,
AMP.6: and you also are among those who are called of Jesus Christ to belong to Him;
AMP.7: [I am writing] to all who are beloved of God in Rome, called to be saints (God's people) and set apart for a sanctified life, [that is, set apart for God and His purpose]: Grace to you and peace [inner calm and spiritual well-being] from God our Father and from the Lord Jesus Christ.
AMP.8: First, I thank my God through Jesus Christ for all of you, because your faith [your trust and confidence in His power, wisdom, and goodness] is being proclaimed in all the world.
AMP.9: For God, whom I serve with my spirit by preaching the gospel of His Son, is my witness as to how continuously I mention you
AMP.10: in my prayers; always pleading that somehow, by God's will, I may now at last come to you.
AMP.11: For I long to see you so that I may share with you some spiritual gift, to strengthen and establish you;
AMP.12: that is, that we may be mutually encouraged and comforted by each other's faith, both yours and mine.
AMP.13: I do not want you to be unaware, brothers and sisters, that many times I have planned to come to you, (and have been prevented so far) so that I may have some fruit [of my labors] among you, even as I have among the rest of the Gentiles.
AMP.14: I have a duty to perform and a debt to pay both to Greeks and to barbarians [the cultured and the uncultured], both to the wise and to the foolish.
AMP.15: So, for my part, I am ready and eager to preach the gospel also to you who are in Rome.
AMP.16: I am not ashamed of the gospel, for it is the power of God for salvation [from His wrath and punishment] to everyone who believes [in Christ as Savior], to the Jew first and also to the Greek.
AMP.17: For in the gospel the righteousness of God is revealed, both springing from faith and leading to faith [disclosed in a way that awakens more faith]. As it is written and forever remains written, "The just and upright shall live by faith."
AMP.18: For [God does not overlook sin and] the wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness and unrighteousness of men who in their wickedness suppress and stifle the truth,
AMP.19: because that which is known about God is evident within them [in their inner consciousness], for God made it evident to them.
AMP.20: For ever since the creation of the world His invisible attributes, His eternal power and divine nature, have been clearly seen, being understood through His workmanship [all His creation, the wonderful things that He has made], so that they [who fail to believe and trust in Him] are without excuse and without defense.
AMP.21: For even though they knew God [as the Creator], they did not honor Him as God or give thanks [for His wondrous creation]. On the contrary, they became worthless in their thinking [godless, with pointless reasonings, and silly speculations], and their foolish heart was darkened.
AMP.22: Claiming to be wise, they became fools,
AMP.23: and exchanged the glory and majesty and excellence of the immortal God for an image [worthless idols] in the shape of mortal man and birds and four-footed animals and reptiles.
AMP.24: Therefore God gave them over in the lusts of their own hearts to [sexual] impurity, so that their bodies would be dishonored among them [abandoning them to the degrading power of sin],
AMP.25: because [by choice] they exchanged the truth of God for a lie, and worshiped and served the creature rather than the Creator, who is blessed forever! Amen.
AMP.26: For this reason God gave them over to degrading and vile passions; for their women exchanged the natural function for that which is unnatural [a function contrary to nature],
AMP.27: and in the same way also the men turned away from the natural function of the woman and were consumed with their desire toward one another, men with men committing shameful acts and in return receiving in their own bodies the inevitable and appropriate penalty for their wrongdoing.
AMP.28: And since they did not see fit to acknowledge God or consider Him worth knowing [as their Creator], God gave them over to a depraved mind, to do things which are improper and repulsive,
AMP.29: until they were filled (permeated, saturated) with every kind of unrighteousness, wickedness, greed, evil; full of envy, murder, strife, deceit, malice and mean-spiritedness. They are gossips [spreading rumors],
AMP.30: slanderers, haters of God, insolent, arrogant, boastful, inventors [of new forms] of evil, disobedient and disrespectful to parents,
AMP.31: without understanding, untrustworthy, unloving, unmerciful [without pity].
AMP.32: Although they know God's righteous decree and His judgment, that those who do such things deserve death, yet they not only do them, but they even [enthusiastically] approve and tolerate others who practice them.

MSG.1: I, Paul, am a devoted slave of Jesus Christ on assignment, authorized as an apostle to proclaim God's words and acts. I write this letter to all the Christians in Rome, God's friends.
MSG.2: The sacred writings contain preliminary reports by the prophets
MSG.3: on God's Son. His descent from David roots him in history;
MSG.4: his unique identity as Son of God was shown by the Spirit when Jesus was raised from the dead, setting him apart as the Messiah, our Master.
MSG.5: Through him we received both the generous gift of his life and the urgent task of passing it on to others who receive it by entering into obedient trust in Jesus.
MSG.6: You are who you are through this gift and call of Jesus Christ!
MSG.7: And I greet you now with all the generosity of God our Father and our Master Jesus, the Messiah.
MSG.8: I thank God through Jesus for every one of you. That's first. People everywhere keep telling me about your lives of faith, and every time I hear them, I thank him.
MSG.9: And God, whom I so love to worship and serve by spreading the good news of his Son—the Message!—knows that every time I think of you
MSG.10: in my prayers, which is practically all the time, I ask him to clear the way for me to come and see you.
MSG.11: The longer this waiting goes on, the deeper the ache. I so want to be there to deliver God's gift in person and watch you grow stronger right before my eyes!
MSG.12: But don't think I'm not expecting to get something out of this, too! You have as much to give me as I do to you.
MSG.13: Please don't misinterpret my failure to visit you, friends. You have no idea how many times I've made plans for Rome. I've been determined to get some personal enjoyment out of God's work among you, as I have in so many other non-Jewish towns and communities. But something has always come up and prevented it.
MSG.14: Everyone I meet—it matters little whether they're mannered or rude, smart or simple—deepens my sense of interdependence and obligation.
MSG.15: And that's why I can't wait to get to you in Rome, preaching this wonderful good news of God.
MSG.16: It's news I'm most proud to proclaim, this extraordinary Message of God's powerful plan to rescue everyone who trusts him, starting with Jews and then right on to everyone else!
MSG.17: God's way of putting people right shows up in the acts of faith, confirming what Scripture has said all along: "The person in right standing before God by trusting him really lives."
MSG.18: But God's angry displeasure erupts as acts of human mistrust and wrongdoing and lying accumulate, as people try to put a shroud over truth.
MSG.19: But the basic reality of God is plain enough. Open your eyes and there it is!
MSG.20: By taking a long and thoughtful look at what God has created, people have always been able to see what their eyes as such can't see: eternal power, for instance, and the mystery of his divine being. So nobody has a good excuse.
MSG.21: What happened was this: People knew God perfectly well, but when they didn't treat him like God, refusing to worship him, they trivialized themselves into silliness and confusion so that there was neither sense nor direction left in their lives.
MSG.22: They pretended to know it all, but were illiterate regarding life.
MSG.23: They traded the glory of God who holds the whole world in his hands for cheap figurines you can buy at any roadside stand.
MSG.24: So God said, in effect, "If that's what you want, that's what you get." It wasn't long before they were living in a pigpen, smeared with filth, filthy inside and out.
MSG.25: And all this because they traded the true God for a fake god, and worshiped the god they made instead of the God who made them—the God we bless, the God who blesses us. Oh, yes!
MSG.26: Worse followed. Refusing to know God, they soon didn't know how to be human either—women didn't know how to be women, men didn't know how to be men.
MSG.27: Sexually confused, they abused and defiled one another, women with women, men with men—all lust, no love. And then they paid for it, oh, how they paid for it—emptied of God and love, godless and loveless wretches.
MSG.28: Since they didn't bother to acknowledge God, God quit bothering them and let them run loose.
MSG.29: And then all hell broke loose: rampant evil, grabbing and grasping, vicious backstabbing. They made life hell on earth with their envy, wanton killing, bickering, and cheating. Look at them: mean-spirited, venomous,
MSG.30: fork-tongued God-bashers. Bullies, swaggerers, insufferable windbags! They keep inventing new ways of wrecking lives. They ditch their parents when they get in the way.
MSG.32: And it's not as if they don't know better. They know perfectly well they're spitting in God's face. And they don't care—worse, they hand out prizes to those who do the worst things best!

NIV.1: Paul, a servant of Christ Jesus, called to be an apostle and set apart for the gospel of God—
NIV.2: the gospel he promised beforehand through his prophets in the Holy Scriptures
NIV.3: regarding his Son, who as to his human nature was a descendant of David,
NIV.4: and who through the Spirit of holiness was declared with power to be the Son of God by his resurrection from the dead: Jesus Christ our Lord.
NIV.5: Through him and for his name’s sake, we received grace and apostleship to call people from among all the Gentiles to the obedience that comes from faith.
NIV.6: And you also are among those who are called to belong to Jesus Christ.
NIV.7:
To all in Rome who are loved by God and called to be saints:
Grace and peace to you from God our Father and from the Lord Jesus Christ.
NIV.8:
Paul’s Longing to Visit Rome
First, I thank my God through Jesus Christ for all of you, because your faith is being reported all over the world.
NIV.9: God, whom I serve with my whole heart in preaching the gospel of his Son, is my witness how constantly I remember you
NIV.10: in my prayers at all times; and I pray that now at last by God’s will the way may be opened for me to come to you.
NIV.11: I long to see you so that I may impart to you some spiritual gift to make you strong—
NIV.12: that is, that you and I may be mutually encouraged by each other’s faith.
NIV.13: I do not want you to be unaware, brothers, that I planned many times to come to you (but have been prevented from doing so until now) in order that I might have a harvest among you, just as I have had among the other Gentiles.
NIV.14: I am obligated both to Greeks and non-Greeks, both to the wise and the foolish.
NIV.15: That is why I am so eager to preach the gospel also to you who are at Rome.
NIV.16: I am not ashamed of the gospel, because it is the power of God for the salvation of everyone who believes: first for the Jew, then for the Gentile.
NIV.17: For in the gospel a righteousness from God is revealed, a righteousness that is by faith from first to last, just as it is written: “The righteous will live by faith.”
NIV.18:
God’s Wrath Against Mankind
The wrath of God is being revealed from heaven against all the godlessness and wickedness of men who suppress the truth by their wickedness,
NIV.19: since what may be known about God is plain to them, because God has made it plain to them.
NIV.20: For since the creation of the world God’s invisible qualities—his eternal power and divine nature—have been clearly seen, being understood from what has been made, so that men are without excuse.
NIV.21: For although they knew God, they neither glorified him as God nor gave thanks to him, but their thinking became futile and their foolish hearts were darkened.
NIV.22: Although they claimed to be wise, they became fools
NIV.23: and exchanged the glory of the immortal God for images made to look like mortal man and birds and animals and reptiles.
NIV.24: Therefore God gave them over in the sinful desires of their hearts to sexual impurity for the degrading of their bodies with one another.
NIV.25: They exchanged the truth of God for a lie, and worshiped and served created things rather than the Creator—who is forever praised. Amen.
NIV.26: Because of this, God gave them over to shameful lusts. Even their women exchanged natural relations for unnatural ones.
NIV.27: In the same way the men also abandoned natural relations with women and were inflamed with lust for one another. Men committed indecent acts with other men, and received in themselves the due penalty for their perversion.
NIV.28: Furthermore, since they did not think it worthwhile to retain the knowledge of God, he gave them over to a depraved mind, to do what ought not to be done.
NIV.29: They have become filled with every kind of wickedness, evil, greed and depravity. They are full of envy, murder, strife, deceit and malice. They are gossips,
NIV.30: slanderers, God-haters, insolent, arrogant and boastful; they invent ways of doing evil; they disobey their parents;
NIV.31: they are senseless, faithless, heartless, ruthless.
NIV.32: Although they know God’s righteous decree that those who do such things deserve death, they not only continue to do these very things but also approve of those who practice them.

NRSVCE.1: Paul, a servant of Jesus Christ, called to be an apostle, set apart for the gospel of God,
NRSVCE.2: which he promised beforehand through his prophets in the holy scriptures,
NRSVCE.3: the gospel concerning his Son, who was descended from David according to the flesh
NRSVCE.4: and was declared to be Son of God with power according to the spirit of holiness by resurrection from the dead, Jesus Christ our Lord,
NRSVCE.5: through whom we have received grace and apostleship to bring about the obedience of faith among all the Gentiles for the sake of his name,
NRSVCE.6: including yourselves who are called to belong to Jesus Christ,
NRSVCE.7: To all God’s beloved in Rome, who are called to be saints: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
NRSVCE.8: First, I thank my God through Jesus Christ for all of you, because your faith is proclaimed throughout the world.
NRSVCE.9: For God, whom I serve with my spirit by announcing the gospel of his Son, is my witness that without ceasing I remember you always in my prayers,
NRSVCE.10: asking that by God’s will I may somehow at last succeed in coming to you.
NRSVCE.11: For I am longing to see you so that I may share with you some spiritual gift to strengthen you —
NRSVCE.12: or rather so that we may be mutually encouraged by each other’s faith, both yours and mine.
NRSVCE.13: I want you to know, brothers and sisters, that I have often intended to come to you (but thus far have been prevented), in order that I may reap some harvest among you as I have among the rest of the Gentiles.
NRSVCE.14: I am a debtor both to Greeks and to barbarians, both to the wise and to the foolish
NRSVCE.15: — hence my eagerness to proclaim the gospel to you also who are in Rome.
NRSVCE.16: For I am not ashamed of the gospel; it is the power of God for salvation to everyone who has faith, to the Jew first and also to the Greek.
NRSVCE.17: For in it the righteousness of God is revealed through faith for faith; as it is written, “The one who is righteous will live by faith.”
NRSVCE.18: For the wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness and wickedness of those who by their wickedness suppress the truth.
NRSVCE.19: For what can be known about God is plain to them, because God has shown it to them.
NRSVCE.20: Ever since the creation of the world his eternal power and divine nature, invisible though they are, have been understood and seen through the things he has made. So they are without excuse;
NRSVCE.21: for though they knew God, they did not honor him as God or give thanks to him, but they became futile in their thinking, and their senseless minds were darkened.
NRSVCE.22: Claiming to be wise, they became fools;
NRSVCE.23: and they exchanged the glory of the immortal God for images resembling a mortal human being or birds or four-footed animals or reptiles.
NRSVCE.24: Therefore God gave them up in the lusts of their hearts to impurity, to the degrading of their bodies among themselves,
NRSVCE.25: because they exchanged the truth about God for a lie and worshiped and served the creature rather than the Creator, who is blessed forever! Amen.
NRSVCE.26: For this reason God gave them up to degrading passions. Their women exchanged natural intercourse for unnatural,
NRSVCE.27: and in the same way also the men, giving up natural intercourse with women, were consumed with passion for one another. Men committed shameless acts with men and received in their own persons the due penalty for their error.
NRSVCE.28: And since they did not see fit to acknowledge God, God gave them up to a debased mind and to things that should not be done.
NRSVCE.29: They were filled with every kind of wickedness, evil, covetousness, malice. Full of envy, murder, strife, deceit, craftiness, they are gossips,
NRSVCE.30: slanderers, God-haters, insolent, haughty, boastful, inventors of evil, rebellious toward parents,
NRSVCE.32: They know God’s decree, that those who practice such things deserve to die — yet they not only do them but even applaud others who practice them.

NET.1: From Paul, a slave of Christ Jesus, called to be an apostle, set apart for the gospel of God.
NET.2: This gospel he promised beforehand through his prophets in the holy scriptures,
NET.3: concerning his Son who was a descendant of David with reference to the flesh,
NET.4: who was appointed the Son-of-God-in-power according to the Holy Spirit by the resurrection from the dead, Jesus Christ our Lord.
NET.5: Through him we have received grace and our apostleship to bring about the obedience of faith among all the Gentiles on behalf of his name.
NET.6: You also are among them, called to belong to Jesus Christ.
NET.7: To all those loved by God in Rome, called to be saints: Grace and peace to you from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ!
NET.8: First of all, I thank my God through Jesus Christ for all of you, because your faith is proclaimed throughout the whole world.
NET.9: For God, whom I serve in my spirit by preaching the gospel of his Son, is my witness that I continually remember you
NET.10: and I always ask in my prayers, if perhaps now at last I may succeed in visiting you according to the will of God.
NET.11: For I long to see you, so that I may impart to you some spiritual gift to strengthen you,
NET.12: that is, that we may be mutually comforted by one another’s faith, both yours and mine.
NET.13: I do not want you to be unaware, brothers and sisters, that I often intended to come to you (and was prevented until now), so that I may have some fruit even among you, just as I already have among the rest of the Gentiles.
NET.14: I am a debtor both to the Greeks and to the barbarians, both to the wise and to the foolish.
NET.15: Thus I am eager also to preach the gospel to you who are in Rome.
NET.16: For I am not ashamed of the gospel, for it is God’s power for salvation to everyone who believes, to the Jew first and also to the Greek.
NET.17: For the righteousness of God is revealed in the gospel from faith to faith, just as it is written, “The righteous by faith will live.”
NET.18: For the wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness and unrighteousness of people who suppress the truth by their unrighteousness,
NET.19: because what can be known about God is plain to them, because God has made it plain to them.
NET.20: For since the creation of the world his invisible attributes — his eternal power and divine nature — have been clearly seen, because they are understood through what has been made. So people are without excuse.
NET.21: For although they knew God, they did not glorify him as God or give him thanks, but they became futile in their thoughts and their senseless hearts were darkened.
NET.22: Although they claimed to be wise, they became fools
NET.23: and exchanged the glory of the immortal God for an image resembling mortal human beings or birds or four-footed animals or reptiles.
NET.24: Therefore God gave them over in the desires of their hearts to impurity, to dishonor their bodies among themselves.
NET.25: They exchanged the truth of God for a lie and worshiped and served the creation rather than the Creator, who is blessed forever! Amen.
NET.26: For this reason God gave them over to dishonorable passions. For their women exchanged the natural sexual relations for unnatural ones,
NET.27: and likewise the men also abandoned natural relations with women and were inflamed in their passions for one another. Men committed shameless acts with men and received in themselves the due penalty for their error.
NET.28: And just as they did not see fit to acknowledge God, God gave them over to a depraved mind, to do what should not be done.
NET.29: They are filled with every kind of unrighteousness, wickedness, covetousness, malice. They are rife with envy, murder, strife, deceit, hostility. They are gossips,
NET.30: slanderers, haters of God, insolent, arrogant, boastful, contrivers of all sorts of evil, disobedient to parents,
NET.32: Although they fully know God’s righteous decree that those who practice such things deserve to die, they not only do them but also approve of those who practice them.
